input.environment: Windows SMB estate with domain-joined hosts
input.objective: Identify share abuse paths, credential exposure, and relay opportunities
command_observations: rpcclient> lookupsids S-1-5-21-...-500  # resolve SID to name
crackmapexec smb <target> -u admin -p pass --sam                 # SAM dump

recon_plan.objective: Identify share abuse paths, credential exposure, and relay opportunities
expected_artifacts: RPC and share metadata reveal user/group and domain structure
SMB signing posture indicates relay feasibility on selected hosts
Historical GPP artifacts may leak decryptable credentials

recon_plan.objective: Confirm exploitability and remove false positives
commands: rpcclient> lookupsids S-1-5-21-...-500  # resolve SID to name
crackmapexec smb <target> -u admin -p pass --sam                 # SAM dump
expected_artifacts: SMB signing not required
Sensitive share overexposure

findings: RPC and share metadata reveal user/group and domain structure
SMB signing posture indicates relay feasibility on selected hosts
Historical GPP artifacts may leak decryptable credentials
hypotheses: Primary hypothesis: RPC and share metadata reveal user/group and domain structure which suggests a viable attack path within windows smb estate with domain-joined hosts.
Alternative hypothesis: observed evidence may represent defensive testing noise rather than active weakness; validate with targeted tool calls.
Operational hypothesis: chaining discovered weaknesses could enable privilege escalation or data access if left unmitigated.
uncertainties: Enumeration evidence is point-in-time and may miss transient services or policy changes
Exploitability is inferred and must be validated in a controlled, authorized test step
Some findings may be false positives caused by legacy artifacts or stale configuration
reasoning.explanation: The selected enumeration sequence supports offensive discovery and vulnerability triage, then transitions to validation and mitigation planning to reduce operational risk.

tool_calls.name: smb_surface_mapper
tool_calls.purpose: Enumerate shares, signing mode, and auth posture

tool_calls.name: ntlm_relay_feasibility_check
tool_calls.purpose: Assess relay preconditions across SMB targets

tool_calls.name: share_secret_hunter
tool_calls.purpose: Locate credential-bearing files and risky artifacts

immediate_actions: Enforce SMB signing and disable SMBv1 where still enabled
Restrict anonymous/null session access and lock down share ACLs
Rotate credentials found in scripts, configs, or GPP artifacts
hardening: Adopt least-privilege share model with regular access recertification
Remove legacy protocols and enforce NTLM hardening policy
Separate admin shares from user-accessible segments
monitoring: Alert on bulk share enumeration and unusual ADMIN$ access
Monitor NTLM authentication anomalies and relay indicators
Track creation/execution patterns of PsExec-like service artifacts
